mentions.note: Caller Sherry (re: flying cars/Joby). Cramer not flying car guy but Boeing well-positioned with aerospace tech for that theme. Down here = attractive entry.

Formerly Alliance Data Systems. Lumpy subprime fintech trap. Earnings swing wildly ($15.95→$4.47→$14.74→$7.60→$12.16 EPS 2020–25). Delinquency 5.8%, charge-offs 2x general cards. Cheap at <8x forward but cheapest right before credit blows. Consumer weakening already forcing tighter underwriting. Prefer Amex (affluent), Capital One (scale), or Affirm (BNPL pure-play).

Multiple engines: infrastructure money, construction capex, data center backup generators. Can string thousands of Cat engines to power data centers off natural gas patches without raising electric rates.
Caller Alex bullish on industrial/concrete/steel transformation. Cramer prefers Nucor—stronger momentum, clearer path higher.

Down 4% despite best-in-class AI integration (Einstein, Claude partnership, Slack communication). Guilt-by-association SaaS sell-off; market pricing in AI worker displacement (fewer workers = lower software spend).

Anthropic's Mythos finds zero-day vulnerabilities; CrowdStrike essential partner in Project Chimera. Security accelerates AI adoption, not disrupts cyber. 15-year data moat from endpoints + cloud = defensible vs. SaaS disruption. Symbiotic, not predatory relationship with AI labs.
Worst performer in Dow today; oil -15% on ceasefire. Live by oil, die by oil. Leverage works both ways in energy sector.

Freed from Topgolf anchor (60% stake sold to Leonard Green for ~$1B valuation Nov 2025; 40% retained). Announced $200M buyback. Stock ran $12→$16→$14.30; now 34x forward earnings. Overvalued vs. Acushnet; better entry was before completion. Has run; sit tight.
At multi-year lows. 6.67% yield does not stop decline. Comparable Smucker reported well but got crushed immediately. Consumer staples facing structural headwinds. No reason to recommend; pass.

Up 59% YoY, 480% since IPO. Titleist (balls), FootJoy (shoes), Scotty Cameron (putters), Vokey (wedges) brands dominate. Post-pandemic golf boom solid; 20% EPS growth expected 2026. Pricey at 26.5x forward but deserves premium for consistency. Wait for pullback unless willing to pay up.

Multiple reasons to own: pro-deal Trump admin accelerates M&A advisory business for investment banks. Significant deal dry powder set to deploy. Reports next week expected strong; CNBC Investing Club has owned long-term.
Hit 2-year low yesterday but surged 5%+ today on ceasefire/rate-cut hopes. Housing still in worst transaction volume in decades; wait for persistent rate cuts before committing.
Last quarter suspect but pullback buying justified. Small decline on Iran news provides entry opportunity; fundamentals intact.
Down 5% despite tax season (unusual); caught in AI/SaaS panic. Not expensive historically. Sellers aggressive—unclear why; sentiment shifted sharply.
Cramer not a flying car guy; been right to avoid. Boeing is better play for aerospace/eVTOL tech exposure.
Caller Michael. Cramer doesn't know stock; needs to do work. Pass for now.

Caller Blake. Cramer owns for travel trust; down 32% YoY, one of his bigger mistakes. Elliott Hill's turnaround happening but too slowly. No floor signs yet. Wait for catalyst/turnaround proof before buying more.
Had great session today. Cramer sees straight shot to $200. Preferred over Commercial Metals. Wishes he'd recommended for travel trust; may add.
Caller Bill. Cramer surprised Anthropic won't hurt it (also insulated from Open AI). Had big 2025 move, now digesting. Not changing mind; holding conviction.
Caller Gabriel question. Cramer likes brand/management (knows Gary Friedman) but balance sheet weak. Never recommends weak balance sheets after 21 years—they always hurt. Pass.
Cramer recommends just buying Bitcoin instead; don't complicate with RIOT. Keep it simple.
Rate-sensitive play; housing market thawing narrative supported by ceasefire calm, though transactional volume still weak—needs persistent rate cuts to confirm floor.
Down significantly; possible connection to coming SpaceX/Starlink IPO, which could disrupt terrestrial telecom. Margin pressure emerging.
Down 6.5%; caught in SaaS selloff narrative that AI gremlins can duplicate HR/finance software. Narrative existential threat likely overblown but market pricing it in.
